---
title: "Porting status"
toc: true
---

What is currently implemented, by upstream source library, for both the shared C++ core (the
faithful port itself) and the R/Python packages (`hecfdar` / `hecfdapy`). "Internal" means the
core carries a validated port that the packages use under the hood but that has no public
wrapper of its own yet. Every "Ported" row is validated by oracle fixtures replayed against the
real HEC-FDA C# libraries.

Status as of v0.1.0.

## HEC.FDA.Statistics

| Area | C++ core | Packages | Notes |
|------|----------|----------|-------|
| Distributions (13 families) | Ported | Public | `dist_pdf`/`dist_cdf`/`dist_quantile`/`dist_sample`; Normal, Uniform, Triangular, Deterministic, LogNormal, TruncatedNormal, TruncatedLogNormal, PearsonIII, LogPearson3, TruncatedLogPearson3, Gamma, ShiftedGamma, Empirical |
| Special functions (gamma/beta closure) | Ported | Internal | `reg_incomplete_gamma`, `log_gamma`, beta family |
| Sample statistics | Ported | Internal | including the upstream unsorted-median bug, reproduced faithfully |
| Validation subsystem | Ported | Internal | `ErrorLevel` + property rules |
| Convergence criteria | Ported | Internal | drives the Monte Carlo iteration bounds |
| Dynamic histograms | Ported | Internal | consequence binning + empirical conversion |
| .NET `System.Random` | Ported (bit-exact) | Public | `rng_sequence`; the foundation of cross-language reproducibility |

: {tbl-colwidths="[30,14,14,42]"}

## HEC.FDA.Model (numerical core)

| Area | C++ core | Packages | Notes |
|------|----------|----------|-------|
| Paired-data curve algebra | Ported | Internal | compose/multiply/integrate, the faithful .NET `Array.BinarySearch`, graphical uncertainty |
| Structures and occupancy types | Ported | Internal (via `stage_damage`) | value/ratio/first-floor-elevation uncertainty samplers, inventory |
| Stage-damage compute | Ported | Public | `stage_damage()`; deterministic path oracle-validated (`TractableStageDamageTests`) |
| EAD Monte Carlo simulation | Ported | Public | `ead_simulation()`; seeded per-curve constants reproduce the C# bit-for-bit (121194.5159789352 at 100 iterations) |
| Performance metrics (AEP, assurance, levee fragility) | Ported | Partial | `ead_simulation()` returns `mean_aep` (default-threshold AEP); assurance and levee metrics stay internal |
| Scenarios (impact-area fan-out) | Ported | Public | `scenario_results()` |
| Alternatives (EqAD annualization) | Ported | Public | `alternative_ead()`, `eqad()` |
| Comparison report (with/without benefits) | Ported | Public | `alternative_comparison()` |

: {tbl-colwidths="[30,14,14,42]"}

## Not ported (severed by design)

HEC-FDA surfaces outside the numerical core were severed from day one, not deferred:
RAS-Mapper terrain and inundation reading, GIS/`Spatial`, RAS-grid hydraulics ingest,
SQLite/DBF persistence, XML serialization, `LifeLoss` (LifeSim), and the WPF/MVVM application
layer. Users supply already-extracted inputs (stage-frequency curves, per-frequency hydraulic
stage profiles, structure inventory tables) as plain arrays and data frames.
